data.problem: A bakery is selling loaves of bread and pies. On the first day of sales, they sold 40 loaves of bread and 5 pies for a total of $340. They took in $640 for 40 loaves of bread and 20 pies on the second day. Find the price of each type of item.
data.solution_steps:
\begin{aligned}
\text{Let } b &\text{ = price of loaf of bread} \\ 
\text{Let } p &\text{ = price of pie} \\ 
 \\ 
40b + 5p &= 340 \\ 
40b + 20p &= 640 \\ 
 \\ 
\text{Subtract equations:} & \\ 
15p &= 300 \\ 
p &= 20 \\ 
 \\ 
\text{Substitute back:} & \\ 
40b + 5(20) &= 340 \\ 
40b + 100 &= 340 \\ 
40b &= 240 \\ 
b &= 6
\end{aligned}

\begin{aligned}
\text{Let } l &\text{ = price of latte} \\ 
\text{Let } m &\text{ = price of muffin} \\ 
 \\ 
40l + 5m &= 345 \\ 
40l + 10m &= 450 \\ 
 \\ 
\text{Subtract equations:} & \\ 
5m &= 105 \\ 
m &= 21 \\ 
 \\ 
\text{Substitute back:} & \\ 
40l + 5(21) &= 345 \\ 
40l + 105 &= 345 \\ 
40l &= 240 \\ 
l &= 6
\end{aligned}
